messages.content: Hi, Evie , this is the spreadsheet I made, for the date 29/30, we didn't capture "check in" "check out" "who to see" on the looseleaf here, so I left blank. And today Bev told me Kevin said he could help with the spreadsheet. check the grammar

Hi Evie, I have created a spreadsheet for the date 29/30. However, we were unable to capture the information for "check in", "check out", and "who to see" on the looseleaf. Therefore, I have left those columns blank. Additionally, Bev mentioned that Kevin has offered to assist with the spreadsheet.

Hi Evie, I have created a spreadsheet for the date 29/30/31. However, we were unable to capture the information for “check in”, “check out”, and “who to see” on the looseleaf on 29/30. Therefore, I have left those columns blank. Additionally, Bev mentioned that Kevin has offered to assist with the spreadsheet. check grammr

Hi Evie, I have created a spreadsheet for the dates 29/30/31. However, we were unable to obtain the details for "check in", "check out" and "who to see" on the looseleaf for the dates 29/30. Therefore, I have left those columns empty. I also want to inform you that Bev told me Kevin volunteered to help with the spreadsheet.
Hi Cathie, just want to let you know that Keith said he's done for the day, he already locked back gate, however one of the hoses was leaking, but he fixed it. check grammar
Hi Cathie, I wanted to inform you that Keith has finished his work for today and has locked the back gate. However, there was a leak in one of the hoses, but he has fixed it.
Hi Cathie, Keith just wants you to let you know that he's done for the day, he already locked back gate, however one of the hoses was leaking, but he fixed it. check grammar

Hi Cathie, Keith just wants you to let you know that he’s done for the day, he already locked back gate, however one of the hoses was leaking, but he fixed it.  is grammar correct?

Hi Cathie, Keith has informed me that he is finished for the day and has already locked the back gate. However, he noticed that one of the hoses was leaking, but he was able to fix it. The grammar is not incorrect, but the use of "just wants" may sound somewhat informal.

Hi Cathie, Keith asked me to inform you that he has finished his work for the day and has locked the back gate. However, he noticed a leak in one of the hoses, but he was able to fix it. check grammar

Lornel is here, and he wants to know that does somebody know grand for home improvement. check grammar

Lornel is here and he wants to know if anybody knows about grants for home improvement. The sentence could be rephrased as "Lornel is here and has asked if anyone knows about grants for home improvement."
